text: Aster Flow Legging. A brushed, high-stretch knit with a smooth high rise that stays put through hip hinges and long walks alike, cut for a women's frame with a clean seam line down the leg. Built as an all-season workhorse for $98, in Black, Storm Blue, and Deep Plum. Wash cold and skip the dryer to keep the compression honest.

Aster Vent Tech Tee. Perforated back panels and a featherweight sweat-wicking knit make this the tee for hot runs and full-sun training. The women's cut is slightly relaxed through the body with a curved hem that clears the hip. $78 in Slate, White, or Ember Red; air-dry and it will hold its shape for seasons.

Aster Daytrip Belt Bag. A compact, unisex crossbody in tough water-resistant nylon with an adjustable strap that sits at hip or chest, sized for phone, keys, and a card sleeve. At $38 it earns a place year-round, whether on a trail or an errand loop. Comes in Black, Sand, and Olive; wipe clean with a damp cloth.

Aster Cloud Hoodie. Midweight fleece with a brushed interior that feels lived-in from the first wear, cut for women with a slightly cropped body and roomy hood. It is the right layer for mild mornings, over a tank on the walk to class or post-workout. $118 in Heather Grey, Black, or Oatmeal. Machine wash cold, inside out, to keep the face fabric smooth.

Aster Storm Shell Jacket. A fully seam-sealed rain shell with a stiff-brimmed hood and articulated sleeves, tailored to a women's fit that layers cleanly over a hoodie. The face fabric sheds steady rain without the crinkle of a cheap poncho. $178, in Storm Blue, Black, and Forest; reproof the DWR yearly rather than replacing it. For commuters and hikers who refuse to check the forecast.

Aster Trailhead Puffer. Warm without the bulk: baffled synthetic insulation that keeps loft even when damp, in a women's cut with a longer back hem and a high storm collar. Built for genuinely cold days, from trailheads to bus stops. $228 in Black, Sand, and Navy. Store it uncompressed in the off-season so the fill stays lofty.

Aster Base Merino Long Sleeve. Fine-gauge merino that regulates warmth and stays fresh through multiple cold-weather wears, cut close to a women's frame with thumb-friendly cuffs for layering under a shell. Works as a base layer on winter runs or on its own with joggers. $88 in Oatmeal, Black, or Forest; wash cold and lay flat to dry.

Aster Studio Sports Bra. A light-support women's bra in a buttery, quick-drying knit with a scoop neck and wide underband that will not dig during hot yoga or a sweaty studio session. The strappy back looks intentional under an open-back tank. $58, in Black, Slate, or Deep Plum. Rinse soon after heavy sweat sessions to protect the elastic.

Aster Momentum Short. A women's running short in a whisper-light woven fabric with a curved side split for full stride and a wide, no-roll waistband. Made for hot-weather miles when anything heavier feels like too much. $68 in Black, Navy, or Sand; the interior drop-in pocket holds a key or gel. Dries fast enough to wash and wear the next morning.

Aster Everywhere Jogger. Structured enough for a coffee meeting, soft enough for a rest day: a midweight double-knit women's jogger with a tapered leg, tailored waistband, and zip hip pockets. Best in mild weather where it can be the whole outfit's anchor. $108 in Charcoal, Black, or Oatmeal. Hang to dry to keep the taper crisp.

Aster Commute Tote. A unisex work-to-gym tote in coated, weather-shrugging canvas with a padded laptop sleeve, an interior zip pocket, and a base that stands up on its own. Carries a 15-inch laptop plus shoes and a change of clothes without looking like a gym bag. $128, in Black, Charcoal, or Sand; spot clean only.

Aster Peak Beanie. A close-ribbed, double-layer knit beanie that covers the ears fully and holds its shape instead of stretching into a slouch, sized unisex. The quiet answer to cold-morning runs and lift lines alike. $38 in Charcoal, Black, or Oatmeal. Hand wash and reshape flat; it pairs with basically every jacket in the line.

Aster Storm Shell Jacket. Une veste de pluie aux coutures entierement etanchees, avec une capuche a visiere rigide et des manches articulees, taillee pour une coupe femme qui se superpose facilement sur un sweat a capuche. Le tissu exterieur repousse la pluie continue sans le froissement d'un poncho bon marche. 178 dollars, en Storm Blue, Black et Forest ; reimpermeabilisez le traitement DWR chaque annee plutot que de la remplacer. Pour les navetteurs et les randonneurs qui refusent de consulter la meteo.

Aster Base Merino Long Sleeve. Un merinos a mailles fines qui regule la chaleur et reste frais apres plusieurs portes par temps froid, coupe pres du corps pour une silhouette femme, avec des poignets a passe-pouce pour se superposer sous une coquille. Ideal comme premiere couche pour les courses hivernales ou seul avec un jogging. 88 dollars, en Oatmeal, Black ou Forest ; lavez a froid et sechez a plat.
